title: Rough Tangy Korean Lentils Jjigae
description: A tangy, anti-inflammatory Korean jjigae with crispy tofu and lentil spheres, perfect for kids.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup French lentils under cold water until clear.
In a pot, bring 4 cups water to boil, add lentils, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While lentils cook, whisk together 2 tbsp gochujang, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp ground ginger, 1 minced garlic clove, and 1 tsp salt to form the tangy broth.
When lentils are tender, drain and stir in the broth mixture; simmer for 5 minutes to meld flavors.
To spherify lentil balls, dissolve 1 tsp sodium alginate in 500 ml water, stir until smooth, and prepare a 0.5% calcium chloride bath by mixing 2 tbsp CaCl2 with 500 ml water.
Drop spoonfuls of the lentil mixture into the calcium bath, let spheres form for 2 minutes, then transfer them to an ice water bowl.
Heat 2 tbsp sesame oil in a skillet, add cubed tofu, and fry until golden and crispy, about 4 minutes; set aside.
Drain the lentil spheres gently and return them to the pot.
Add the crispy tofu cubes to the soup, and simmer for an additional 3 minutes.
Serve the jjigae hot, optionally garnishing with extra sesame seeds and sliced green onions.
